steps.title: Identify Text 1's central barrier
steps.content: Text 1's key signal is that cost is the critical obstacle to mycoprotein adoption. The author emphasizes that while mycoprotein is environmentally sound, its expense restricts availability, and biochemists are exploring cost solutions like cheaper substrates.

steps.title: Determine the author's response to the findings
steps.content: Choice B correctly articulates the connection: a Text 1 author concerned with the cost barrier would respond to Text 2's environmental data by asserting that cheaper substrates are essential to achieving those benefits. The other choices either shift focus from cost (A to water, C to consumer preference, D to regional patterns) or don't address Text 1's central concern. That matches choice B — B is correct.
